**This entire repository — including all code, configuration, and ADRs — was generated by AI (Claude) as a demonstration.**

# Backstage ADR Demo

A [Backstage](https://backstage.io) developer portal with [Architecture Decision Records](https://adr.github.io/) (ADR) plugin support. ADRs are stored in `docs/adr/` using the [MADR](https://adr.github.io/madr/) format and rendered in the Backstage catalog via the ADR tab.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 22+
- A [GitHub personal access token](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens) with `repo` scope

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repo:

   ```sh
   git clone git@github.com:brandisher/backstage-adrs.git
   cd backstage-adrs
   ```

2. Install dependencies (Yarn is bundled in the repo):

   ```sh
   node .yarn/releases/yarn-4.4.1.cjs install
   ```

3. Set your GitHub token. The ADR plugin reads ADR files from GitHub, so a valid token is required:

   ```sh
   export GITHUB_TOKEN=<your-github-pat>
   ```

4. Start the app:

   ```sh
   node .yarn/releases/yarn-4.4.1.cjs start
   ```

5. Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser. Navigate to the **backstage-adr-test** component in the catalog and click the **ADRs** tab.

## ADRs

ADR files live in `docs/adr/` and follow MADR format with sequential numbering (`0001-*.md`). To add a new ADR, create a file following the existing pattern, commit, and push. The ADR plugin will pick it up automatically.

## Project Structure

```
docs/adr/              # Architecture Decision Records
packages/app/          # Backstage frontend
packages/backend/      # Backstage backend
examples/              # Example catalog entities
catalog-info.yaml      # Root catalog entity with ADR annotation
app-config.yaml        # Backstage configuration
```
